sourceMovie.title: Roald Dahl's The Witches
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Roald Dahl's The Witches for the same reason it broke through in 2020: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Robert Zemeckis keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The House with a Clock in Its Walls
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Roald Dahl's The Witches clicked for you, The House with a Clock in Its Walls (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: How the Grinch Stole Christmas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Roald Dahl's The Witches clicked for you, How the Grinch Stole Christmas (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Roald Dahl's The Witches?
faq.answer: Try The House with a Clock in Its Walls, How the Grinch Stole Christmas, Ella Enchanted—each shares DNA with Roald Dahl's The Witches while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Roald Dahl's The Witches?
faq.answer: Roald Dahl's The Witches (2020) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy, Family, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Roald Dahl's The Witches worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
